name: German A1 Vocabulary
description: Beginner German — CEFR A1 level vocabulary

facts.explanation: Abend — evening; the time from dusk until midnight. Also: The west
abendessen — to sup, to have supper (the evening meal).
Abfahrt — departure (of a land or water vehicle). Also: exit, slip road, off-ramp (lane used to leave a motorway), slope, piste, downhill (fastest of the four alpine competitions)
Absicht — intention. Also: intent
Achtung — attention. Also: esteem, respect
Adresse — address.
AG — initialism of Arbeitgeber. Also: initialism of Auftraggeber
Ahnung — anticipation, inkling. Also: clue, idea, notion
Akkusativ — the accusative (case).
Aktivität — activity.
Akzent — accent (modulation of the voice). Also: accent (stress mark)
Alkohol — alcohol, ethanol (substance). Also: alcohol, liquor (beverage containing the former substance)
All — cosmos.
Altbau — old building.
Ampel — traffic light. Also: ellipsis of Ampelkoalition, ceiling lamp (lamp which hangs from the ceiling), container (e.g. for a plant) which hangs from the ceiling
Anfang — start, beginning, origin (spatially or temporally).

Angebot — offer, proposition. Also: a range of products or services provided by a company or institution, supply, bargain, sale, a product, service, facility, or activity made available to consumers or users
Angst — fear; fright; anxiety.
Animateur — holiday representative, club representative, entertainer.
Ankunft — arrival.
Anrede — salutation, form of address (e.g., in a letter). Also: a (short) speech to someone
Anruf — a call upon, an appeal (e.g., to gods, a court, etc.). Also: a call, a holler, a (phone) call
Anrufbeantworter — answering machine.
Anschluss — connection, joining. Also: annexation, Anschluss, contact
Antwort — answer.
Anweisung — directive, order. Also: instructions
Anzeige — advertisement, announcement. Also: display, charge, notification (of the authorities)
Apfel — apple (fruit). Also: apple tree, breasts, abbreviation of Pferdeapfel (“road apple”)
Apfelsaft — apple juice.
Apotheke — pharmacy.

Apparat — apparatus, device, machine (complex instrument). Also: apparat, apparatus (organisation, especially bureaucratic), the entirety of an editor's critical notes to a text, (loosely also) other appended information, such as indices etc., short for Telefonapparat (“telephone”)
Appetit — appetite.
Arabisch — Arabic language.

Arbeit — toil, regularly performed work, regularly pursued economic activity, labor, job, employment, occupation. Also: job, task, assignment, effort, work, human expenditure, performance, workmanship, work, the result of effort that might consist in a product or performance itself depending on what is owed, work, energy transmitted by force through a body
Arbeitnehmer — employee, worker (chiefly in the context of labor contracts and the labor market, male or of unspecified gender).
Arbeitskollege — colleague, coworker.
Arbeitsplatz — workplace. Also: place of employment, employment, job, position
Arbeitstag — workday, working day.
Arbeitszeit — working hours (the time of duty at a workplace, the time spend working).
Arbeitszimmer — workroom, study (room for work, typically in a private house).
Architekt — architect (male or of unspecified gender).
Architektur — architecture.
Arm — an arm. Also: anything that resembles an arm, a sleeve, a long protusion on a device or machine, e.g. a lever, a branch, a section of a structure, an arm of a creek or a river, influence, might
Artikel — article. Also: article, item, ware, article, paragraph, clause
Arzt — doctor, physician (male or of unspecified gender) (without necessarily having a postgraduate degree).
Arzttermin — doctor's appointment, medical appointment.
Assistentin — A female assistant..
Atmosphäre — atmosphere. Also: atmosphere, ambiance, mood
Attraktion — attraction.
Aufbau — construction (the manner in which or process by which something is built). Also: buildup

Aufgabe — task, job. Also: duty, responsibility, mission, function, purpose, chore, assignment, to-do, assignment, exercise, problem (e.g. at school), corresponding to other senses of the verb aufgeben, abandonment, relinquishment (of resistance, a claim etc.), surrender, the act of giving up, posting (act of sending mail)
Auflauf — crowd. Also: cobbler (sweet baked dish), (typically layered) baked dish or casserole (for example, a gratin)
Auge — eye. Also: germ, bud; eye (potato), dot, pip, spot, drop or globule of grease or fat
Ausbildung — training, apprenticeship, vocational education, education.
Ausdruck — expression, term. Also: diction, expression, manifestation, manner
Ausflug — excursion, outing, short trip.
Auskunft — enquiry. Also: information, information desk
Ausland — foreign countries (collectively).
Ausländer — foreigner; foreign national, regardless of descent (male or of unspecified gender). Also: allochthone person, regardless of nationality (male or of unspecified gender)
Aussicht — scenery, view, outlook (also figuratively).
Aussprache — pronunciation. Also: discussion, debate, talk
Auto — car.
Autobahn — motorway (Britain, Ireland, New Zealand), freeway (Australia, Canada, US), highway (parts of the US), expressway (parts of Canada, parts of the US), controlled-access highway.
Automechaniker — auto mechanic.
Backofen — oven.
Bad — bath. Also: bathroom, pool, baths, spa; (health) resort
Badeanzug — swimsuit; bathing suit; bathing costume (garment worn for swimming, covering at least part of the torso).
Badehose — A pair of swimming trunks.
Badewanne — bathtub.
Badezimmer — bathroom (a room in a private dwelling where people wash themselves, containing a shower and/or bathtub).

Bahn — route, trail. Also: railway/railroad or rail transport, short for Eisenbahn a vehicle in rail transport (especially a regional commuter train or tram, otherwise more often Zug or Tram), ellipsis of Autobahn, ellipsis of Fahrbahn (“lane [of a road]”), A large, wide sheet of material used for waterproofing roofs and other structures; waterproofing membrane, orbit
Bahnhof — railway depot, railroad station, railway station, train station.
Balkon — balcony (an accessible structure extending from a building, especially outside a window or overlooking a stage).
Banane — banana (fruit or tree).
Bank — bench (which people sit on); pew. Also: workbench (which things can be set down on), bank (collection of material in a body of water), substitutes' bench
bar — in cash. Also: pure
Bart — beard. Also: bit (the part of a key which interacts with the lock)
Bauch — abdomen, belly.
Bauchschmerz — abdominal pain, bellyache, stomachache, tummyache.
Bauernhof — A farm..
Baum — tree. Also: boom
befinden — to occupy a place; to be located; to be situated. Also: to find, to consider
Bein — leg of a person, animal, or object. Also: bone
Beispiel — example.
Bericht — report, account, news story.
Beruf — occupation, profession, trade, job, career. Also: vocation
Bescheid — notification, information.
Besichtigung — inspection. Also: viewing, sightseeing
besondere — special, particular, extraordinary.
Besprechung — meeting, discussion, (gathering among business people to discuss their business). Also: Review
Besserung — amelioration. Also: improvement
Bestellung — order, request for a product.
Besuch — visit, call. Also: visitor or visitors
Besucher — visitor. Also: patron, the visitor design pattern
Betreff — subject, reference (of e-mails and letters).
Bett — bed (piece of furniture, usually flat and soft, for resting or sleeping on; one's place of sleep or rest). Also: bed (bottom of a body of water, such as an ocean, sea, lake, or river)
Bewegung — motion. Also: move (step made in the execution of a plan or goal; a change in strategy), movement
Bewerbung — application (for a job or position), candidature. Also: promotion (of a product, etc.)
Bezahlung — payment.
Bezirk — district, borough (formal administrative division).
Bibliothek — library.
Bier — beer (alcoholic beverage fermented from starch material; a serving of this beverage). Also: business, beeswax (personal affairs)
Biergarten — beer garden.
Bild — image; picture; painting; photo (optical representation of something). Also: image (idea or mental concept of something), image
Biografie — biography.
Biologie — biology.
Birne — pear. Also: lightbulb, head, bonce, noggin
bitte — please, if you please (used to make a polite request or affirm an offer).
Bleistift — pencil (writing utensil with a graphite shaft).
Blick — glance, look. Also: view

Blume — flower, blossom (colorful, conspicuous reproductive structure(s) of an angiosperm). Also: plant, houseplant (plant that is grown indoors in places such as a house or office for decorative purposes), efflorescence (formation of a powdery surface on crystals), flower (symbol representing a flower), tail, scut (short, erect tail of a hare), nose, bouquet (scent of a particular wine), head (foam that forms on top of beer)
Bluse — blouse.
Brief — letter (written message).

Brille — glasses, spectacles (frames bearing two lenses worn in front of the eyes to correct vision). Also: goggles (protective eyewear set in a flexible frame to fit snugly against the face), toilet seat (hinged, contoured seat with a hole in the middle, of a toilet), nasal cannula for oxygen (clear plastic tubes for the delivery of oxygen to the nose)
Brot — bread. Also: loaf of bread, slice of bread; sandwich, livelihood, subsistence
Bruder — brother. Also: Brother (title of respect)
Brötchen — a bun, bread roll. Also: a small open sandwich
Brücke — bridge. Also: The bridge pattern, pons (in the brain, "bridging" the brain stem with the rest of the brain)
Bundeskanzleramt — chancellery, especially:. Also: the Chancellery of Germany, the Chancellery of Austria
Burg — castle, fortification. Also: beaver lodge, sandcastle
Bäckerei — bakery, baker’s, bakehouse, bakeshop. Also: biscuits, small pastry
Bär — bear (animal). Also: bear (someone or something bear-like, e.g. a sturdy man)
Bühne — a stage. Also: a scaffolding
Büro — office (room for working). Also: office, bureau, agency (administrative department or service provider), the staff of such places
Café — a café, a coffee shop. Also: coffee
CD-Player — CD player.
Cent — cent, the smaller unit of the euro. Also: cent (in other currencies)
Chef — boss, chief.
Chefin — female boss.
Chiffre — cipher. Also: box number
Chinesisch — Chinese. Also: jargon (often used as the second element in compounds, e.g. “Patentchinesisch” is used for “patentese”)
Cola — Coke, Coca-Cola, cola (beverage made with caramel and carbonated water).
Computerprogramm — computer program.
Cousin — male cousin.
Cousine — female cousin.
Dame — lady (woman of good breeding and manners). Also: lady; madam (polite term to refer to any woman), queen, king, Dame
Dank — thanks, gratitude, appreciation.
Dativ — the dative (case).
Datum — date. Also: datum, a fact, a piece of information about something
Deckel — lid, cap (the cover of a container). Also: ellipsis of Bierdeckel (“beer mat”), headwear, hat, cap (artificial upper limit or ceiling)
deutsch — German (of or pertaining to the German people). Also: German (of or pertaining to Germany), German (of or pertaining to the German language)
Deutschkurs — German language course.
Deutschlehrer — German teacher (male or of unspecified gender).
Deutschlehrerin — German teacher (female).
Dezember — December.
Dialog — dialogue.
Dienst — service. Also: work, duty
Dienstag — Tuesday.

Ding — thing. Also: thing; girl; boy (young person), Thing (historic Germanic council)
DJ — DJ, disc jockey.
Doktor — doctor. Also: doctor, physician
Dom — cathedral (church serving as seat of a bishop, by extension, any large church).
Donnerstag — Thursday.
Dorf — village (rural habitation of size between a hamlet and a town). Also: backwater (remote place; somewhere that remains unaffected by new events, progresses, ideas, etc.)
Dose — box (container made from metal or plastic, less often wood). Also: tin, can (air-tight container for food), vagina, vulva
Du — the thou, the you (singular).
Durst — thirst.
Dusche — a shower (for bathing).
E-Mail-Adresse — e-mail address.

Ecke — corner, edge. Also: a roughly triangular or quadrangular piece of something, corner of a house / of two streets, region, area, neighbourhood, bit, tad, vertex, node, corner, corner kick, one of the two areas of a goal near where the post and goalline meet; bottom or lower corner
Ehemann — married man, husband.
Ehepaar — married couple.
Ei — egg (all biological senses). Also: something egg-shaped, testicle, ball, balls, guts, courage, bucks (money), clown; foolish bloke
Einbauküche — built-in kitchen, fitted kitchen.

Eingang — entrance, entry. Also: arrival, input, entrance gate, doorway, hall, passage, orifice, inlet, mouth (of a river), introduction, importation, access, beginning, preface, preamble, prologue, receipt
Einladung — invitation.

Einsatz — something inserted, inset, inlay, compartment. Also: insertion, use, usage, employment, mission, campaign, deployment, appearance (instance of playing for a team), effort, commitment, the act of risking (something for some end), stake, the amount of a bet or wager, deposit, start (of something), entry, entrance, cue

Eintritt — occurring; occurrence (the fact that something happens). Also: joining; enlistment (becoming a member, e.g. of a club), entrance, access (right to go in, also the fee thereof), entry, entrance (door or area where one enters)

Einwohner — inhabitant (anyone who lives or dwells in something, especially a country, region, settlement). Also: an inhabitant without the full rights of a citizen; now used in the form Inwohner in historic terminology
Einwohnermeldeamt — registration office.
Eis — ice. Also: ice cream
Eistee — iced tea, ice tea (cold tea).
Eltern — parents.
Ende — end, finish. Also: conclusion
Energie — energy.
englisch — English. Also: rare
Enkelkind — grandchild.

Erdgeschoss — first floor (US), ground floor (UK).
Erfolg — success.
Ergebnis — result, outcome, conclusion, finding, fruit, consequence, upshot, answer. Also: earnings, profit, output, outturn, score
Erkältung — cold (illness).
erste — first.
Es — E-flat.
essen — to eat. Also: to eat; to dine
Esszimmer — dining room (room, in a home or hotel, where meals are eaten).
Etage — floor; storey.
Europäer — European (person).
Exkursion — excursion, field trip.
Fahrgast — passenger (in road- or rail-based public transport).
Fahrkarte — fare ticket.

Fahrplan — timetable (for transport).
Fahrrad — bicycle.
Fahrradtour — bike tour.
Fahrt — drive. Also: ride, journey

Fall — fall, drop (the act of falling or an instance thereof). Also: fall, capture (the act of being seized by enemy forces), fall; the loss of one's innocence, honour, reputation, fortune, etc., case (actual event, situation, or fact), case (instance or event as a topic of study), case (piece of work subject to case handling in an authority, court, customer support etc.), case (instance of a specific condition or set of symptoms), case (kind of inflection of a nominal)
Familie — family.
Fan — fan, devotee.

Fasching — The week before Mardi Gras, carnival..
Faxe — shenanigan, nonsense.
Februar — February.
Fehler — fault, error, mistake.
Feier — celebration. Also: party, ceremony, service, observance
Feierabend — The evening before a holiday, eve.. Also: Quitting time, hometime; the workday's end and the evening following it, used for relaxation or leisure.
Fenster — window. Also: time frame
Ferien — holidays during which an institution (especially a school, university) or a business is closed; break (usually three days or more). Also: vacation, holiday
Fernsehen — television.

Fieber — fever (heightened body temperature). Also: vigour, confidence, fever, bug (obsession for an activity)

Also: wife (married woman, especially in relation to her spouse), a title of courtesy, equivalent to Mrs/Ms, which has nearly replaced Fräulein in the function of Miss, madam (polite form of address for a woman or lady), lady; noblewoman (woman of breeding or higher class)
Freiheit — freedom. Also: liberty, clearance
Freitag — Friday.
Freitagvormittag — Friday morning (time before noon).
Freizeit — free time, leisure time, spare time. The time during which one is not working..
Fremdsprache — foreign language.
Freude — joy, delight, glee.

Frühjahr — spring.

Frühstück — breakfast.
Fuß — foot (body part). Also: footing, pedestal, foot (unit of length; always in the singular), metrical foot, leg

Führerschein — driver's license (US), driver's licence (Aus), driving licence (UK).

Geburtstag — birthday.

Geld — money.
Geldautomat — ATM, automated teller machine.
Gemüse — vegetable; vegetables (kinds of plants). Also: a seasoned vegetable-based side dish, such as a relish (not necessarily pickled and not usually in the form of a paste)
Gepäck — luggage, baggage.
Gericht — court (of justice). Also: judgement / judgment
Geschenk — present, gift.

Gesundheit — health (state of being in good physical condition and free from illness). Also: health; soundness; strength; stability (state of being in good condition)
Getränk — drink, beverage (liquid for consumption).
Gewitter — thunderstorm, lightning storm.
Gitarre — guitar.
Glas — glass. Also: jar (made of glass)
Gleis — railway (track on which trains run). Also: track

Gott — god.

Gramm — gram (unit of mass).

Großeltern — grandparents.

Hallo — hullabaloo, cheering (often welcoming or celebratory).
Hals — neck. Also: throat

Halsschmerz — sore throat.
Haltestelle — stop (place to get on and off line buses or trams).
Handel — deal. Also: trade, trading
Handschuh — any handwear; glove, mitten, mitt, gauntlet.
Handy — mobile phone, mobile, cell phone, phone (portable, wireless telephone).
Handynummer — mobile number; mobile phone number.
Hast — haste.
Hauptbahnhof — central/main train station.
Hauptsache — main thing, most important thing. Also: main issue
Hauptstadt — capital (city).
Haus — house. Also: home (in various phrases), theatre

Hausfrau — housewife.

Hausmeister — caretaker or janitor (male or of unspecified gender) (someone who repairs and potentially cleans a building).
Haustür — front door. Also: doorstep, threshold
Haut — skin, hide of a person, animal or (part of a) plant. Also: a creature, especially a person, skin (membrane found on the surface of an object, like a sausage)
Hefe — yeast.

Heft — notebook, writing booklet, cahier, composition book (book in which notes or memoranda are written). Also: notepad, writing pad (pad of paper on which one jots down notes), exercise book (booklet for students, containing problems and exercises, or blank pages for writing answers), number, issue (single edition of a periodical publication), magazine (non-academic periodical publication), comic (magazine that uses sequences of drawings to tell a story or series of stories)
Heilkraut — medicinal herb.
Heimat — home, home town, homeland, native land. Also: home; homeland; place where something originated or where it is deep-rooted
Heimatland — homeland, home country.
Heimatstadt — hometown.
Heizung — heating (a system that raises the temperature of a room or building). Also: radiator
Hemd — shirt, or in particular a dress shirt.
Herbst — autumn, fall.
Herd — cooker; stove. Also: hob; cooktop, fireplace, hearth, the household as the traditional workplace of women, hotbed, place where something (negative) spreads from
Herkunft — origin, source. Also: descent (lineage or hereditary derivation), etymology (origin of a word)

Herr — man, gentleman. Also: sir, lord; address of subservient respect, Mr., mister, sir respectful address towards a man, master, lord (generally denotes that someone has control over something, either in a generic or in a regal sense), the lowest title of German nobility, Lord, God
Herz — heart. Also: hearts, sweetheart, darling
heute — today.

hier — here; in this place; refers to an environment one is currently in, or to something within reach. Also: there; right there; in that place; refers to something that is considered nearby, or can be seen and pointed at, here (within this context), used with the definite article or the determiner dieser (“this, that”) to express proximity (“this”)

Hochhaus — skyscraper, high-rise building (very tall building with a large number of floors).

Hof — an independent town in the Upper Franconia region, Bavaria. Also: a rural district of the Upper Franconia region, Bavaria, surrounding but not including the town of Hof, which nevertheless serves as its administrative seat, a municipality of Westerwaldkreis district, Rhineland-Palatinate, a town in Bruck an der Leitha district, Lower Austria; official name: Hof am Leithaberge, a municipality of Salzburg-Umgebung district, Salzburg; official name: Hof bei Salzburg, a village in Holmestrand municipality, Vestfold county, Norway
Honig — honey.
Hose — trousers.
Hotelzimmer — hotel room.

husten — to cough.
Hut — hat. Also: head, top
Händler — trader, merchant.
Höhe — height, altitude.

hören — to hear (to perceive sounds (or a sound) through the ear). Also: to hear, to listen to, pay attention to (to give (someone) one's attention), to attend, to go to, to sit in on, to get, to receive, to listen (to pay attention to a sound or speech; to accept advice or obey instruction), to hear (to receive information; to come to learn), to hear (to be contacted (by))
Ich — ego. Also: self, me, him, etc.
Idee — idea.
in — in, popular (in fashion).

Italienerin — female Italian (person).

JA — initialism of Junge Alternative für Deutschland..

Jahreszahl — date (of the year).
Januar — January.
Jeans — A pair of jeans, denim trousers.
jetzt — now (at this moment, right now). Also: now, then (expressing a logical or temporal consequence), now (unstressed and expletive, used for minor emphasis)
Job — job, position, employment. Also: job, work, profession, job, task, assignment
joggen — to jog. Also: to get oneself into a certain state by jogging
Joghurt — yogurt.

Jugendherberge — youth hostel (a supervised, inexpensive lodging place, primarily for young people).
Juli — July.

Juni — June.
